macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Close Lower on March 19 with VIX Spike
items.summary: S&P 500 fell 0.27% to 6606.49, Nasdaq 100 down 0.29% to 24355.275, and Dow down 0.44% to 65876.58 amid lighter volume and VIX rising to 25.09, signaling heightened investor anxiety.

items.title: S&P 500 Lower on March 20 Amid Higher Oil Prices
items.summary: Stock market live updates indicate S&P 500 (SPY) trading lower due to rising oil prices for the second day.

items.title: Silver Prices Plunge Nearly 10% on March 19
items.summary: Spot silver dropped nearly 10% intraday to around $73.21 per ounce, driven by stronger US dollar, rising bond yields, and Fed policy uncertainty post-FOMC meeting.

items.title: Fed Holds Rates, Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast to 2.7%
items.summary: Jerome Powell confirmed rates on hold with updated inflation projection of 2.7% from 2.4%, attributing the increase to Middle East energy shocks.
items.source: Capital Street FX Crypto Market Analysis

items.title: Apple Closes Down 0.39% on March 19
items.summary: Apple Inc. stock ended at $248.96, down 0.98 (-0.39%), with flat after-hours trading; Q1 2026 financial results discussed in market position analysis.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Ethereum Price Rises to $2,142.77 Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Ethereum (ETH) reached $2,142.77 at 8:45 a.m. ET on March 20, 2026, marking a $21.01 increase from yesterday and an 80% gain earlier in the year despite sharp declines due to recession fears and sales by co-founder Vitalik Buterin.

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs to $70,416.89 After Recent Dip Below $70K
items.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) traded at $70,416.89 at 8:45 a.m. ET on March 20, 2026, up $1,046.75 from yesterday following an intraday low of $68,834 on March 19 amid higher inflation forecasts from Fed and ECB.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips Below $70K on Central Bank Hawkishness and Oil Surge
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to $68,834 on March 19 as Fed and ECB raised 2026 inflation forecasts due to energy shocks from Middle East conflict, with Brent crude briefly topping $119; markets now price fewer rate cuts.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits Critical Resistance After Losing Key Support
items.summary: Bitcoin encountered critical 4-hour resistance following a major breakdown, with the daily chart losing its most bullish support for the first time, as noted in the March 20 morning summary.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops 5% Post-Fed Announcement on Zero Rate Cuts
items.summary: Bitcoin declined about 5% in the 24 hours after a Fed announcement where seven officials projected zero rate cuts this year, aligning with historical sell-the-news patterns.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026 Keynote Reveals Next-Gen AI Infrastructure
items.summary: NVIDIA's Jensen Huang unveiled major AI breakthroughs and live demos of next-generation AI infrastructure during the GTC 2026 keynote in San Jose, pushing hardware boundaries for enterprise AI deployment.

items.title: Jeff Bezos Reportedly Plans $100 Billion Fund for AI Manufacturing
items.summary: Jeff Bezos is seeking $100 billion for a secret AI project aimed at transforming manufacturing, potentially ushering in Industry 7.0.

items.title: Anthropic Captures 73% of New Enterprise AI Spending
items.summary: Anthropic now leads with over 73% of spending among first-time enterprise AI buyers, up from a 50/50 split with OpenAI just 10 weeks ago, signaling a shift toward enterprise monetization.

items.title: White House Releases AI Legislative Recommendations
items.summary: The White House issued recommendations emphasizing child safety, infrastructure, IP, free speech, workforce development, and federal preemption of state AI laws to promote uniform national policy.

items.title: Meta Deploys New AI Content Enforcement Systems
items.summary: Meta rolled out proprietary AI systems for detecting harmful content, reducing reliance on third-party vendors as internal AI outperforms human hybrids for certain moderation tasks.

items.title: CHRO Survey: AI Tops HR Executives' Priorities
items.summary: 91% of CHROs rank AI and workplace digitization as top concerns, with early successes in talent acquisition (30%), HR service delivery (17%), and learning development (14%).

items.title: Cloudflare Warns Bot Traffic to Exceed Human by 2027
items.summary: Cloudflare reports bot traffic will surpass human traffic by 2027, highlighting infrastructure strains amid AI-driven growth.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Confirms Marathon Release in March 2026 at $40
items.summary: Bungie and PlayStation announced that the extraction shooter Marathon will launch in March 2026 for $40, featuring improvements like proximity chat, solo modes, and enhanced visuals based on community feedback following a delay from September 2025.

items.title: Colorado Bill Proposes Loot Box Fees to Fund Public Schools
items.summary: A new Colorado bill aims to direct revenue from online game loot box purchases toward public school funding, targeting in-game microtransactions.

items.title: MarketBeat Highlights Top Video Game Stocks: Turtle Beach, Motorsport Games, Alliance Entertainment
items.summary: Turtle Beach, Motorsport Games, and Alliance Entertainment are flagged as promising video game stocks to watch due to high recent trading volume, covering gaming peripherals, racing titles, and entertainment distribution.

items.title: Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse 'On Schedule' with New Day One Xbox Game Pass Title
items.summary: Updates include Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse remaining on schedule and a new game launching day one on Xbox Game Pass, alongside One Piece games for Nintendo Switch.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: NVIDIA Unveils Next-Gen Hardware Roadmap at GTC 2026 Including Vera CPU and Feynman GPU
items.summary: NVIDIA announced a strategic pivot toward AI inference and agentic workflows, revealing its next-generation roadmap with the high-performance Vera CPU, Feynman GPU architecture for 2028, and DLSS 5 neural rendering for gaming.

items.title: Broadcom Ships World's First 2nm Custom Compute SoC
items.summary: Broadcom has begun shipping the world's first 2nm custom compute System on Chip, marking a significant advancement in semiconductor manufacturing.

items.title: Qualcomm Authorizes $20 Billion Share Repurchase and Increases Dividend
items.summary: Qualcomm announced a massive $20 billion share repurchase program alongside a dividend increase, signaling strong financial confidence.

items.title: Samsung and ASML Hit Milestones in Sub-2nm Chip Manufacturing
items.summary: Samsung retained its lead in commercial displays while ASML delivered high-NA EUV systems to imec for sub-2nm development; TSMC and Samsung raised prices for sub-5nm nodes due to tight capacity.

items.title: Marvell Launches Next-Generation CXL Switches for Data Centers
items.summary: Marvell introduced new CXL switches designed for AI data centers, enhancing connectivity and performance in high-demand environments.

items.title: AI Data Center Boom Drives Six-Figure Salaries for Skilled Trades Like Electricians
items.summary: Demand for electricians, HVAC technicians, and construction workers is surging due to data center construction, with salaries reaching $240k-$280k for young workers amid a shortage of hundreds of thousands.

items.title: Future Food-Tech SF 2026 Showcases Fermentation and Alternative Food Innovations
items.summary: Startups like Savor, Kokomodo, Cellva, Bettani Farms, Maia Farms, and Michroma unveiled carbon-derived butter, chocolate alternatives, non-dairy cheese, mycelium proteins, and precision-fermented dyes.
